author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXLIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …The title of the Psalm is, “To David himself, when his son was pursuing him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know from the Books of Kings that this happened:…but we must recognise here another David, truly “strong in hand,” which is the explanation of David, even our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all those events of past time were figures of things to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us seek then in this Psalm our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ, announcing Himself beforehand in His prophecy, and foretelling what should happen at this time by things which were done long ago.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He Himself foretold Himself in the Prophets: for He is the Word of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor did they say ought of this kind, save when filled with the Word of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They announced then Christ, being filled with Christ, they went before Him about to come, and He deserted not them going before.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let then our Lord speak; let Christ with us, whole Christ, speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, hear my prayer, receive with Thine ears my entreaty” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear” and “receive with ears” are the same thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is repetition, it is confirmation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thy truth hear me, in Thy righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take it not without emphasis when it is said, “in Thy righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is a commendation of grace, that none of us think his righteousness his own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this is the righteousness of God, which God hath given thee to possess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what saith the Apostle of them, who would boast of their own righteousness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Speaking of the Jews, he saith, “they have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.”…Thou art perverse, because thou imputest what thou hast done ill to God, what well to thyself: thou wilt be right, when thou imputest what thou hast done ill to thyself, what well to God.…Behold, “in Thy righteousness hear me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when I look upon myself, nought else do I find mine own, save sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And enter not into judgment with Thy servant” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are willing to enter into judgment with Him, save they who, “being ignorant of the righteousness of God, go about to establish their own?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Wherefore have we fasted, and Thou hast not seen; wherefore have we afflicted our souls, and Thou takest no knowledge?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As though they would say, “We have done what Thou hast commanded, wherefore dost Thou not render to us what Thou hast promised?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God answereth thee: I will give to thee to receive what I have promised: I have given thee that thou shouldest do that whereby thou mayest receive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Finally, to such proud ones the Prophet speaketh; “Wherefore will ye plead with Me? ye have all transgressed against Me, saith the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why will ye enter into judgment with Me, and recount your own righteousnesses?…“For before Thee every one living shall not be justified.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Every one living;” living, that is, here, living in the flesh, living in expectation of death; born a man; deriving his life of man; sprung from Adam, a living Adam; every one thus living may perhaps be justified before himself, but not before Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How before himself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By pleasing himself, displeasing Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Enter not then into judgment with me, O Lord my God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How straight soever I seem to myself, Thou bringest forth a standard from Thy store-house, Thou fittest me to it, and I am found crooked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Well is it said, “with Thy servant.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is unworthy of Thee to enter into judgment with Thy servant, or even with Thy friend.…What of the Apostles themselves?…That ye may perceive it at once, they learnt to pray what we pray: to them was given the pattern of prayer by the heavenly Counsellor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “After this manner,” saith He, “pray ye.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And having set down certain things first, He laid down this too to be said by the leaders of the sheep, the chief members of the Shepherd and Gatherer of the one flock; even they learnt to say, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They said not, “Thanks be to Thee, who hast forgiven us our debts, as we too forgive our debtors,” but, “Forgive, as we forgive.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But surely the faithful prayed then, surely the Apostles prayed then, for this Lord’s Prayer was given rather to the faithful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If those debts only were meant which are forgiven by Baptism, it would befit catechumens rather to say, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Apostles then say, yea let them say, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when it is said to them, “Wherefore say ye this? what are your debts?” let them answer, “for in Thy sight every one living shall not be justified.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the enemy hath persecuted my soul: he hath humbled my life on the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here we speak, here our Head speaketh for us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Manifestly both the devil persecuted the Soul of Christ and Judas the Soul of his Master: and now too the same devil remaineth to persecute the Body of Christ, and one Judas succeedeth another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There lacketh not then of whom the Body too may say, “For the enemy hath persecuted my soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what doth each one who persecuteth us endeavour save to make us abandon our heavenly hope, and savour of the earth, yield to our persecutor, and love earthly things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have laid me in dark places, as the dead of the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This ye hear more readily from the Head; this ye perceive more readily in the Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He died indeed for us, yet was He not one of the “dead of the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who are the “dead of the world”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how was not He one of the “dead of the world”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The dead of the world” are those who have died of their own desert, receiving the reward of iniquity, deriving death from the sin transmissed to them; according as it is said, “For I was conceived in iniquity.”…In dying, saith He, I do the will of My Father, but I am not deserving of death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nought have I done wherefore I should die, yet is it Mine own doing that I die, that by the death of an innocent One, they may be freed who had wherefore they should die.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They set me in places,” as though in Hades, as though in the tomb, as though in His very Passion, “as the dead of the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And My Spirit within me,” saith He, “suffered weariness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Remember, “My soul is exceeding sorrowful, even unto death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here we see one voice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do we not see plainly the transition from the Head to the members, from the members to the Head?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we too were there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He goes to the members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have called to mind the days of old” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did He “call to mind the days of old,” by whom every day was made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No, but the body speaketh, each one who has been justified by His grace, who dwelleth in Him in love and devout humility, speaketh and saith, “I have meditated upon all Thy works:” plainly because Thou hast made all things good, and nothing would have stood fast, which was not established by Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy creation is made a spectacle unto me: I have sought in the work the Artificer, in all that is made the Maker.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore this, to what purpose this, save that he might understand, that whatever there was of good in himself was made by Him.…Look back then upon the Framer of thy life, the Author of thy substance, of thy righteousness, and of thy salvation: “meditate upon the works of His hands,” for the righteousness too which is in thee, thou wilt find to pertain to His hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle teaching thee this, “not of works,” he saith, “lest any should boast.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have we no good works?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Plainly we have: but see what follows; “for we are His workmanship,” saith he.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We are His workmanship:” perhaps in thus speaking of workmanship, he meant to mention the nature whereby we are men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Evidently not: he was speaking of works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let us not make conjectures; let the text go on, “for we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not then that thou thyself doest anything, save in so far as thou art evil.…“Work out your own salvation,” saith the Apostle, “with fear and trembling.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we do work out our own salvation, wherefore with fear, wherefore with trembling, when what we work is in our own power?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear wherefore with fear and trembling: “for it is God that worketh in you both to will and to do, of His good pleasure.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore “with fear and trembling,” that it may delight our Maker to work in the lowly valley.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I stretched forth,” saith he, “my hands to Thee: my soul is as a land without water to Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rain upon me, saith he, to bring forth from me good fruit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord shall give sweetness, that our land may give her fruit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have stretched forth my hands to Thee; my soul is as a land without water,” not to me, but “to Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I can thirst for Thee, I cannot water myself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Speedily hear me, Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what need of delay to inflame my thirst, when already I thirst so eagerly?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst delay the rain, that I might drink and imbibe, not reject, Thy inflowing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then Thou didst for this cause delay, now give; for “my spirit hath failed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Thy Spirit fill me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the reason why Thou shouldest speedily hear me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am now become “poor in spirit,” make Thou me “blessed in the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he in whom his own spirit liveth, is proud, is puffed up with his own spirit against God.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn not Thou away Thy Face from me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst turn it away from me when proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For once I was full, and in my fulness I was puffed up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Once “in my fulness I said, I shall never be moved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I said in my fulness, I shall not be moved,” knowing not Thy Righteousness, and establishing mine own; but “Thou, Lord, in Thy Will hast afforded strength to my beauty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I said in my fulness, I shall not be moved,” but from Thee came whatever fulness I had.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And to prove to me that it was from Thee, “Thou didst turn away Thy Face from me, and I was troubled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After this trouble, where into I was cast, because Thou didst turn away Thy Face, after the weariness of my spirit, after my heart was troubled within me, because Thou didst turn away Thy Face, then became I “like a land without water to Thee: turn not Thou away Thy Face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou turnedst it away from me when proud; give it back to me now I am humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because, if Thou turn it away, “I shall be like to them that go down into the pit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “that go down into the pit”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the sinner has come into the depth of sins, he will show contempt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They “go down into the pit,” who lose even confession; against which is said, “Let not the pit close her mouth over me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This depth Scripture calleth mostly “a pit,” into which depth when a sinner hath come, “he showeth contempt.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “he showeth contempt”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He no longer believeth in Providence, or if he do believe, he thinketh that he has no longer aught to do with it.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Make me to hear in the morning Thy mercy, for in Thee have I hoped” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, I am in the night, yet “in Thee have I hoped,” until the iniquity of the night pass away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For we have,” as Peter saith, “a more sure word of prophecy, whereunto ye do well that ye take heed, as unto a light that shineth in a dark place, until the day dawn, and the daystar arise in your hearts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Morning” then he calleth the time after the end of the world, when we shall see what in this world we believe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what here, until the morning come?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is not enough to hope for the morning; we must do somewhat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do somewhat?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God is to be sought with the hands in the night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “with the hands”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By good works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then we must thus hope for the morning, and bear this night, and persevere in this patience until the day dawn, what meanwhile must we do here? lest perchance thou think that thou wilt do aught of thyself, whereby thou mayest earn to be brought to the morning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Make known to me, O Lord, the way wherein I must walk.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore did He kindle the lamp of prophecy, therefore did He send the Lord in the vessel, as it were, of the flesh, who should even say, “My strength is dried up like a potsherd.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Walk by prophecy, walk by the lamp of future things predicted, walk by the word of God.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver me from mine enemies, O Lord, for unto Thee have I fled for refuge” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I who once fled from Thee, now flee to Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Adam fled from the Face of God, and hid himself among the trees of Paradise, so that of him was said in the Book of Job, “As a servant that fleeth from his Lord, and findeth a shadow.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He fled from the Face of his Lord, and found a shadow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Woe to him, if he continue in the shade, lest it be said afterward, “All things are passed away like a shadow.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The rulers of this world, of this darkness, the rulers of the wicked; against these ye wrestle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great is your conflict, not to see your enemies, and yet to conquer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against the rulers of this world, of this darkness, the devil, that is, and his angels; not the rulers of that world, whereof is said, “the world was made by Him,” but that world whereof is said, “the world knew Him not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For unto Thee have I fled for refuge.”…Whither should I flee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whither shall I go from Thy Spirit?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Teach me to do Thy will, for Thou art my God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Glorious confession! glorious rule!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou,” saith he, “art my God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To another will I hasten to be re-made, if by another I was made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art my all, “for Thou art my God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall I seek a father to get an inheritance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art my God,” not only the Giver of mine inheritance, but mine Inheritance itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is the portion of mine inheritance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall I seek a patron, to obtain redemption?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art my God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, having been created, do I desire to be re-created?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art my God,” my Creator, who hast created me by Thy Word, and re-created me by Thy Word.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Teach Thou me:” for it cannot be that Thou art my God, and yet I am to be mine own master.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See how grace is commended to us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This hold fast, this drink in, this let none drive out of your hearts, lest ye have “a zeal, of God, but not according to knowledge.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say then this: “Thy good Spirit,” not my bad one, “Thy good Spirit shall lead me into the right land.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For my bad spirit hath led me into a crooked land.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what have I deserved?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What can be reckoned as my good works without Thy aid, through which I might obtain and be worthy to be led by Thy Spirit into the right land?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen, then, with all your power, to the commendation of Grace, whereby ye are saved without price.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thy Name’s sake, O Lord, Thou shalt quicken me in Thy righteousness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 11); not in mine own: not because I have deserved, but because Thou hast mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For were I to show mine own desert, nought should I deserve of Thee, save punishment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast pruned off from me mine own merits; Thou hast grafted in Thine own gifts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt bring forth my soul out of tribulation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in thy mercy shalt bring mine enemies to destruction: and thou shalt destroy all them that afflict my soul; for I am Thy servant” (ver.
